items.caption: A slide from a presentation with a title "coastal deposition." it contains bullet points explaining the concept of coastal deposition. the first bullet point states that coastal deposition takes place in areas where the flow of water slows down. the second bullet point explains that coasts are built up when the amount of deposition is greater than the amount of erosion. the third bullet point states that the energy of the water is lost due to friction against the sea bed, meaning the sediment can no longer be carried or rolled along and has to be deposited. the fourth bullet point states that the amount of material deposited will increase when there is excessive erosion elsewhere on the coast, meaning that there is a lot of material to be transported into the area. the slide has a background image of a beach with clear blue waters and a rocky shore, which is likely intended to illustrate the concept of coastal deposition.
